sentences.sentence: Pheidole dentata, a native ant of the south-eastern U.S., isn't immortal.
sentences.translation: Pheidole dentata 是一种原产于美国东南部的蚂蚁，它并非长生不死。

structure.text: Pheidole dentata
a native ant of the south-eastern U.S.
isn't immortal

difficult_points: 逗号中的 a native ant of the south-eastern U.S. 是 Pheidole dentata 的同位语，解释物种背景。
isn't immortal 是否定表达，先说明这种蚂蚁并非不会死亡。
native ant of... 中 of 引出原产地区。

sentences.sentence: But scientists have found that it doesn't seem to show any signs of aging.
sentences.translation: 但是科学家发现，它似乎没有表现出任何衰老迹象。

structure.text: But scientists have found
that it doesn't seem to show any signs of aging
any signs of aging

difficult_points: But 承接上一句形成转折：虽然会死，却不显老。
that 引导宾语从句，说明 scientists have found 的具体内容。
doesn't seem to show 表示“似乎没有表现出”，语气比直接否定更谨慎。

sentences.sentence: Old worker ants can do everything just as well as the youngsters, and their brains appear just as sharp.
sentences.translation: 年老的工蚁做任何事都能和年轻工蚁一样好，而且它们的大脑看起来同样敏锐。

structure.text: Old worker ants can do everything just as well as the youngsters
just as well as the youngsters
and their brains appear just as sharp

difficult_points: just as well as 构成同级比较，强调老工蚁表现不逊于年轻工蚁。
the youngsters 指年轻的 worker ants。
appear just as sharp 中 sharp 形容大脑敏锐，而不是物理上的锋利。

sentences.sentence: 'We get a picture that these ants really don't decline,' says Ysabel Giraldo, who studied the ants for her doctoral thesis at Boston University.
sentences.translation: “我们看到的情况是，这些蚂蚁确实不会衰退，”伊莎贝尔·吉拉尔多说；她在波士顿大学攻读博士论文时研究了这些蚂蚁。

structure.text: 'We get a picture that these ants really don't decline,'
that these ants really don't decline
says Ysabel Giraldo
who studied the ants for her doctoral thesis at Boston University

difficult_points: 引号内是直接引语，主句 says Ysabel Giraldo 放在引语之后。
get a picture that... 表示“形成一种认识/图景”，that 后解释 picture 的内容。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Giraldo 的研究背景。

sentences.sentence: Such age-defying feats are rare in the animal kingdom.
sentences.translation: 这种抗衰老的本领在动物界很少见。

structure.text: Such age-defying feats
are
rare in the animal kingdom

difficult_points: Such 指上一段所说老蚂蚁不明显衰退的表现。
age-defying 是复合形容词，表示“抵抗年龄影响的”。
in the animal kingdom 限定这种现象在动物界范围内很罕见。

sentences.sentence: Naked mole rats can live for almost 30 years and stay fit for nearly their entire lives.
sentences.translation: 裸鼹鼠可以活将近30年，并且几乎一生都保持健康。

structure.text: Naked mole rats
can live for almost 30 years
and stay fit for nearly their entire lives

difficult_points: can 同时管辖 live 和 stay 两个并列动词。
for almost 30 years 表示寿命持续时间。
for nearly their entire lives 说明保持健康几乎贯穿整个生命。

sentences.sentence: They can still reproduce even when old, and they never get cancer.
sentences.translation: 它们即使年老时仍能繁殖，而且从不会患癌。

structure.text: They can still reproduce even when old
even when old
and they never get cancer

difficult_points: They 指前一句的 Naked mole rats。
even when old 是省略结构，完整意思是 even when they are old。
never get cancer 用 never 强调裸鼹鼠不会患癌这一特征。

sentences.sentence: But the vast majority of animals deteriorate with age just like people do.
sentences.translation: 但是绝大多数动物都会像人类一样随着年龄增长而衰退。

structure.text: But the vast majority of animals
deteriorate with age
just like people do

difficult_points: But 将裸鼹鼠的特殊性与多数动物的常规衰老形成对比。
the vast majority of 表示“绝大多数”，主语核心是 majority。
people do 中 do 代替前面的 deteriorate with age，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Like the naked mole rat, ants are social creatures that usually live in highly organised colonies.
sentences.translation: 像裸鼹鼠一样，蚂蚁是社会性生物，通常生活在高度组织化的群体中。

structure.text: Like the naked mole rat
ants are social creatures
that usually live in highly organised colonies

difficult_points: Like the naked mole rat 是介词短语，表示类比关系。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 social creatures。
highly organised colonies 指内部结构复杂、组织化程度高的群体。

sentences.sentence: 'It's this social complexity that makes P. dentata useful for studying aging in people,' says Giraldo, now at the California Institute of Technology.
sentences.translation: “正是这种社会复杂性使 P. dentata 对研究人类衰老有用，”如今在加州理工学院工作的吉拉尔多说。

structure.text: 'It's this social complexity that makes P. dentata useful for studying aging in people,'
this social complexity
that makes P. dentata useful for studying aging in people
says Giraldo, now at the California Institute of Technology

difficult_points: It's...that... 是强调句型，强调 this social complexity 是有研究价值的原因。
makes P. dentata useful for... 中 useful 是宾语补足语。
now at the California Institute of Technology 是对 Giraldo 当前任职地点的补充说明。

sentences.sentence: Humans are also highly social, a trait that has been connected to healthier aging.
sentences.translation: 人类也具有高度社会性，而这一特征被认为与更健康的衰老有关。

structure.text: Humans are also highly social
a trait
that has been connected to healthier aging

difficult_points: a trait 是 highly social 的同位解释，把“高度社会性”概括为一种特征。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 a trait。
has been connected to 是现在完成时被动语态，表示已有研究把该特征与健康衰老联系起来。

sentences.sentence: By contrast, most animal studies of aging use mice, worms or fruit flies, which all lead much more isolated lives.
sentences.translation: 相比之下，大多数关于衰老的动物研究使用小鼠、蠕虫或果蝇，而这些动物的生活都孤立得多。

structure.text: By contrast
most animal studies of aging use mice, worms or fruit flies
which all lead much more isolated lives

difficult_points: By contrast 标示与前文人类和蚂蚁的社会性形成对比。
which 指 mice, worms or fruit flies 这三类实验动物。
lead much more isolated lives 中 lead 表示“过某种生活”，不是“带领”。

sentences.sentence: In the lab, P. dentata worker ants typically live for around 140 days.
sentences.translation: 在实验室中，P. dentata 工蚁通常能活大约140天。

structure.text: In the lab
P. dentata worker ants
typically live for around 140 days

difficult_points: In the lab 限定寿命数据来自实验室环境，不一定等同野外情况。
typically 表示一般情况或典型情况。
for around 140 days 表示持续时间，around 表示约数。

sentences.sentence: Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges: 20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days.
sentences.translation: 吉拉尔多重点研究了四个年龄范围的蚂蚁：20至22天、45至47天、95至97天以及120至122天。

structure.text: Giraldo focused on ants at four age ranges
at four age ranges
20 to 22 days, 45 to 47 days, 95 to 97 days and 120 to 122 days

difficult_points: focused on 表示研究关注对象。
冒号后面具体列出 four age ranges 的内容。
四组年龄范围构成并列结构，用于比较不同年龄蚂蚁。

sentences.sentence: Unlike all previous studies, which only estimated how old the ants were, her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults, so she knew their exact ages.
sentences.translation: 不同于以往所有只估算蚂蚁年龄的研究，她的研究从蛹变成成虫时就开始追踪这些蚂蚁，因此她知道它们的准确年龄。

structure.text: Unlike all previous studies
which only estimated how old the ants were
her work tracked the ants from the time the pupae became adults
so she knew their exact ages

difficult_points: Unlike 是介词，整个短语表示与以往研究的对比。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 previous studies 的局限。
from the time the pupae became adults 中 the pupae became adults 是省略关系词的时间说明。
so 引出结果：她能掌握准确年龄。

sentences.sentence: Then she put them through a range of tests.
sentences.translation: 随后，她让它们接受了一系列测试。

structure.text: Then
she
put them through a range of tests

difficult_points: Then 标示实验流程的下一步。
them 指被追踪的 P. dentata worker ants。
put them through 表示让它们经历或接受测试，不是字面上的“放进去”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 使它们经历；让它们接受

sentences.sentence: Giraldo watched how well the ants took care of the young of the colony, recording how often each ant attended to, carried and fed them.
sentences.translation: 吉拉尔多观察这些蚂蚁照料群体中幼蚁的情况，并记录每只蚂蚁照看、搬运和喂养它们的频率。

structure.text: Giraldo watched how well the ants took care of the young of the colony
how well the ants took care of the young of the colony
recording how often each ant attended to, carried and fed them

difficult_points: how well 引导 watched 的宾语内容，表示观察照料质量。
the young of the colony 指蚁群中的幼体。
recording 是现在分词短语，说明观察同时记录的内容。
attended to, carried and fed 是三个并列动作，共同说明照料行为。

sentences.sentence: She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food.
sentences.translation: 她比较了20日龄和95日龄蚂蚁跟随那种指示性气味的能力，这种气味通常由昆虫留下，用来标记通往食物的路线。

structure.text: She compared how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent
how well 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants followed the telltale scent
that the insects usually leave to mark a trail to food
to mark a trail to food

difficult_points: compared 后接 how well 引导的宾语从句，比较的是跟踪气味的能力。
20-day-old and 95-day-old 是复合形容词，分别修饰 ants。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 scent。
to mark a trail to food 表示留下气味的目的。

sentences.sentence: She tested how ants responded to light and also measured how active they were by counting how often ants in a small dish walked across a line.
sentences.translation: 她测试蚂蚁对光的反应，还通过计算小盘子里的蚂蚁穿过一条线的频率来测量它们的活跃程度。

structure.text: She tested how ants responded to light
how ants responded to light
and also measured how active they were
by counting how often ants in a small dish walked across a line

difficult_points: tested 和 measured 是并列谓语，描述两项实验测量。
how ants responded to light 与 how active they were 都是宾语从句。
by counting... 表示测量活跃度的方法。
walked across a line 说明计数标准是穿过线的次数。

sentences.sentence: And she experimented with how ants react to live prey: a tethered fruit fly.
sentences.translation: 她还实验了蚂蚁如何对活猎物作出反应：一只被拴住的果蝇。

structure.text: And she experimented with how ants react to live prey
how ants react to live prey
a tethered fruit fly

difficult_points: And 放在句首延续前面列举的实验项目。
how ants react to live prey 是介词 with 的宾语内容。
冒号后的 a tethered fruit fly 具体说明 live prey 是什么。

sentences.sentence: Giraldo expected the older ants to perform poorly in all these tasks.
sentences.translation: 吉拉尔多原以为年老的蚂蚁会在所有这些任务中表现不佳。

structure.text: Giraldo
expected the older ants to perform poorly
the older ants to perform poorly
in all these tasks

difficult_points: expected sb to do sth 表示预期某人会做某事。
perform poorly 表示表现差，和后文实际结果形成对比。
all these tasks 指前文列出的照料、寻迹、对光反应、活跃度和捕食反应等任务。

sentences.sentence: But the elderly insects were all good caretakers and trail-followers — the 95-day-old ants could track the scent even longer than their younger counterparts.
sentences.translation: 但是这些年老的昆虫都是很好的照料者和寻迹者——95日龄的蚂蚁甚至能比更年轻的同类更久地追踪气味。

structure.text: But the elderly insects were all good caretakers and trail-followers
the 95-day-old ants could track the scent even longer than their younger counterparts
even longer than their younger counterparts

difficult_points: But 与上一句预期形成转折，指出实验结果并不差。
破折号后进一步举例说明年老蚂蚁的寻迹能力。
counterparts 指与95日龄蚂蚁对应的年轻蚂蚁。
even longer than 表示程度超出预期。

sentences.sentence: They all responded to light well, and the older ants were more active.
sentences.translation: 它们对光的反应都很好，而且年老的蚂蚁更活跃。

structure.text: They all responded to light well
responded to light
and the older ants were more active

difficult_points: They 指参与测试的蚂蚁。
responded to light well 说明对光反应测试的结果良好。
more active 是比较级，表示年老蚂蚁比年轻蚂蚁活跃度更高。

sentences.sentence: And when it came to reacting to prey, the older ants attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did, flaring their mandibles or pulling at the fly's legs.
sentences.translation: 而在对猎物作出反应方面，年老的蚂蚁攻击那只可怜果蝇的凶猛程度与年轻蚂蚁一样，会张开上颚或拉扯果蝇的腿。

structure.text: And when it came to reacting to prey
the older ants attacked the poor fruit fly just as aggressively as the young ones did
just as aggressively as the young ones did
flaring their mandibles or pulling at the fly's legs

difficult_points: when it came to 是固定表达，表示“在……方面”。
the young ones did 中 did 代替 attacked the poor fruit fly，避免重复。
flaring 和 pulling 是并列现在分词，具体说明攻击方式。
mandibles 指蚂蚁的上颚，是捕食动作的重要部位。

sentences.sentence: Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants, identifying any cells that were close to death.
sentences.translation: 随后，吉拉尔多比较了20日龄和95日龄蚂蚁的大脑，识别其中任何接近死亡的细胞。

structure.text: Then Giraldo compared the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants
the brains of 20-day-old and 95-day-old ants
identifying any cells that were close to death
that were close to death

difficult_points: Then 标示研究从行为测试转向大脑比较。
identifying 是现在分词短语，说明比较大脑时做的进一步观察。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 any cells。
close to death 指细胞接近死亡状态。

sentences.sentence: She saw no major differences with age, nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells, showing that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions.
sentences.translation: 她没有看到随年龄出现的重大差异，濒死细胞的位置也没有任何差异，这表明年龄似乎并未影响特定的大脑功能。

structure.text: She saw no major differences with age
nor was there any difference in the location of the dying cells
showing that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions
that age didn't seem to affect specific brain functions

difficult_points: no major differences 和 nor was there any difference 构成连续否定，强调没有发现年龄差异。
nor 位于句首引起倒装，所以是 nor was there 而不是 nor there was。
showing 是现在分词短语，说明前述发现所支持的结论。
didn't seem to affect 表示谨慎判断：似乎没有影响。

sentences.sentence: Ants and other insects have structures in their brains called mushroom bodies, which are important for processing information, learning and memory.
sentences.translation: 蚂蚁和其他昆虫的大脑中有一种叫作蘑菇体的结构，它们对信息处理、学习和记忆很重要。

structure.text: Ants and other insects have structures in their brains called mushroom bodies
called mushroom bodies
which are important for processing information, learning and memory

difficult_points: called mushroom bodies 是过去分词短语，修饰 structures。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 mushroom bodies 的作用。
processing information, learning and memory 是并列名词性成分，说明其涉及的认知功能。

sentences.sentence: She also wanted to see if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures—regions where neurons come together.
sentences.translation: 她还想看看衰老是否会影响这些结构内突触复合体的密度——也就是神经元汇聚的区域。

structure.text: She also wanted to see
if aging affects the density of synaptic complexes within these structures
within these structures
regions where neurons come together

difficult_points: if 引导宾语从句，表示她要观察的问题是“是否影响”。
the density of synaptic complexes 是 affects 的宾语核心。
破折号后的 regions where neurons come together 解释 synaptic complexes 所指区域。
where 引导定语从句，修饰 regions。

sentences.sentence: Again, the answer was no.
sentences.translation: 同样，答案是否定的。

structure.text: Again
the answer
was no

difficult_points: Again 表示与前面“没有明显差异”的结果一致。
the answer 指上一句关于衰老是否影响突触复合体密度的问题。
was no 是简洁回答，意思是没有影响。

sentences.sentence: What was more, the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine—brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging.
sentences.translation: 而且，年老蚂蚁的血清素或多巴胺水平也没有任何下降——这两种脑化学物质的下降通常与衰老同时出现。

structure.text: What was more
the old ants didn't experience any drop in the levels of either serotonin or dopamine
either serotonin or dopamine
brain chemicals whose decline often coincides with aging

difficult_points: What was more 用来添加进一步证据，意思是“而且”。
didn't experience any drop 表示完全没有下降。
either serotonin or dopamine 表示两种物质中的任何一种。
whose 引导定语从句，修饰 brain chemicals，说明这些化学物质下降通常与衰老相关。

sentences.sentence: In humans, for example, a decrease in serotonin has been linked to Alzheimer's disease.
sentences.translation: 例如在人类中，血清素水平下降已被认为与阿尔茨海默病有关。

structure.text: In humans
for example
a decrease in serotonin
has been linked to Alzheimer's disease

difficult_points: In humans 限定这个例子发生在人类研究中。
a decrease in serotonin 是主语，核心词是 decrease。
has been linked to 是现在完成时被动语态，表示已有研究联系。
for example 插入句中，用来举例说明前一句提到的脑化学物质与衰老的关系。

sentences.sentence: 'This is the first time anyone has looked at both behavioral and neural changes in these ants so thoroughly,' says Giraldo, who recently published the findings in the Proceedings of the Royal Society B.
sentences.translation: “这是第一次有人如此全面地研究这些蚂蚁的行为和神经变化，”吉拉尔多说；她最近在《皇家学会学报B》上发表了这些发现。

structure.text: 'This is the first time anyone has looked at both behavioral and neural changes in these ants so thoroughly,'
anyone has looked at both behavioral and neural changes in these ants so thoroughly
says Giraldo
who recently published the findings in the Proceedings of the Royal Society B

difficult_points: This is the first time 后接现在完成时 has looked，表示到目前为止第一次。
both behavioral and neural changes 是并列宾语，说明研究覆盖行为和神经两个层面。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Giraldo 的发表信息。
the findings 指本研究的发现。

sentences.sentence: Scientists have looked at some similar aspects in bees, but the results of recent bee studies were mixed—some studies showed age-related declines, which biologists call senescence, and others didn't.
sentences.translation: 科学家们曾研究过蜜蜂的一些类似方面，但近期蜜蜂研究的结果并不一致——有些研究显示出与年龄相关的衰退，生物学家称之为衰老，而另一些研究没有显示这种情况。

structure.text: Scientists have looked at some similar aspects in bees
but the results of recent bee studies were mixed
some studies showed age-related declines
which biologists call senescence

difficult_points: have looked at 使用现在完成时，表示相关研究已经做过。
but 引出转折：虽然研究过蜜蜂，但结果并不统一。
破折号后解释 mixed 的具体含义：有些研究显示衰退，有些没有。
which 指 age-related declines，补充其生物学名称 senescence。

sentences.sentence: 'For now, the study raises more questions than it answers,' Giraldo says, 'including how P. dentata stays in such good shape.'
sentences.translation: 吉拉尔多说：“目前，这项研究提出的问题比回答的问题更多，包括 P. dentata 如何保持如此良好的状态。”

structure.text: 'For now, the study raises more questions than it answers,'
Giraldo says
'including how P. dentata stays in such good shape.'
how P. dentata stays in such good shape

difficult_points: 直接引语被 Giraldo says 插入分成两部分，阅读时要把两段引语连起来理解。
more questions than it answers 是比较结构，表示研究仍留下许多未知。
including 后接 how 引导的内容，举出其中一个尚未回答的问题。
stays in such good shape 指保持良好的身体和功能状态。

expressions.text: raise more questions than it answers
expressions.meaning_zh: 提出的问题多于回答的问题

sentences.sentence: Also, if the ants don't deteriorate with age, why do they die at all?
sentences.translation: 此外，如果这些蚂蚁不会随着年龄增长而衰退，那它们究竟为什么会死亡呢？

structure.text: Also
if the ants don't deteriorate with age
why do they die at all

difficult_points: if 引导条件从句，提出疑问的前提。
they 指 P. dentata ants。
at all 用在疑问句中加强语气，表示“究竟、到底”。

sentences.sentence: Out in the wild, the ants probably don't live for a full 140 days thanks to predators, disease and just being in an environment that's much harsher than the comforts of the lab.
sentences.translation: 在野外，由于捕食者、疾病，以及所处环境远比舒适的实验室严酷，这些蚂蚁很可能活不到完整的140天。

structure.text: Out in the wild
the ants probably don't live for a full 140 days
thanks to predators, disease and just being in an environment
that's much harsher than the comforts of the lab

difficult_points: Out in the wild 与实验室环境形成对比。
thanks to 在这里表示原因，后接 predators、disease 和 being in an environment 三个并列原因。
that's 是 that is 的缩写，引导定语从句修饰 environment。
much harsher than... 是比较结构，强调野外环境更严酷。

sentences.sentence: 'The lucky ants that do live into old age may suffer a steep decline just before dying,' Giraldo says, but she can't say for sure because her study wasn't designed to follow an ant's final moments.
sentences.translation: 吉拉尔多说：“那些确实活到老年的幸运蚂蚁可能会在死亡前不久急剧衰退，”但她无法确定，因为她的研究并不是为了追踪一只蚂蚁生命最后时刻而设计的。

structure.text: 'The lucky ants that do live into old age may suffer a steep decline just before dying,'
that do live into old age
but she can't say for sure
because her study wasn't designed to follow an ant's final moments

difficult_points: that do live into old age 修饰 The lucky ants，其中 do 用来强调“确实活到”。
may suffer 表示可能发生，并非确定事实。
but she can't say for sure 说明研究不能支持确定结论。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么无法确定。

sentences.sentence: 'It will be important to extend these findings to other species of social insects,' says Gene E. Robinson, an entomologist at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.
sentences.translation: “把这些发现扩展到其他社会性昆虫物种将很重要，”伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校的昆虫学家 Gene E. Robinson 说。

structure.text: 'It will be important to extend these findings to other species of social insects,'
to extend these findings to other species of social insects
says Gene E. Robinson
an entomologist at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式短语 to extend these findings...。
extend these findings to... 表示把研究发现推广到其他对象上验证。
an entomologist... 是 Gene E. Robinson 的同位语，说明其专业身份。
social insects 指具有社会性组织的昆虫。

sentences.sentence: This ant might be unique, or it might represent a broader pattern among other social bugs with possible clues to the science of aging in larger animals.
sentences.translation: 这种蚂蚁可能是独特的，也可能代表其他社会性昆虫中更广泛的一种模式，并为研究较大型动物衰老的科学提供可能线索。

structure.text: This ant might be unique
or it might represent a broader pattern among other social bugs
with possible clues to the science of aging in larger animals

difficult_points: might 表示可能性，说明作者对结论保持谨慎。
or 连接两种解释：该蚂蚁独一无二，或代表更广泛模式。
it 指 This ant，即 P. dentata。
with possible clues... 补充说明 broader pattern 的潜在研究意义。

sentences.sentence: Either way, it seems that for these ants, age really doesn't matter.
sentences.translation: 无论哪种情况，对这些蚂蚁来说，年龄似乎确实并不重要。

structure.text: Either way
it seems
that for these ants, age really doesn't matter
for these ants

difficult_points: Either way 总结上一句两种可能性，表示“不论哪种情况”。
it seems that... 中 it 是形式主语，that 从句承载真正内容。
for these ants 是插入的适用范围，说明“年龄不重要”只针对这些蚂蚁。
doesn't matter 表示没有影响或不重要。
